Release Notes:

What’s new in 3.0.0

  • Configuration is delivered through Splunk UCC (Universal Configuration Console).
  • SSL certificate (PEM) for trusting the Claroty API can be set in the add-on UI (global settings).


Description:
The Claroty Continuous Threat Detection (CTD) add-on for Splunk delivers comprehensive security visibility and alert management capabilities for operational technology (OT) environments. Designed to be used in tandem with both Splunk ES and the Splunk Add-on for OT Security, the app enables Splunk to automatically ingest OT asset data, events, and alerts from Claroty CTD. As a result, users can monitor all assets and potential threats in their OT environment on a single pane of glass in real-time, leading to more effective and efficient OT security monitoring and stronger OT security posture.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dunphy

item.title: PSTree for Splunk - v2.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Updated splunk-sdk-python to 2.1.1 version


Description:
This apps main function is to enable a custom Splunk search command to reconstruct a pstree from Sysmon process creation events (EventCode 1). Information from memory forensics, such as Volatility's pstree, can be very helpful to detect malicious processes. By ingesting Sysmon events in Splunk and using this command you can quickly get similar information without performing memory forensics.
item.dc:creator: Donald Murchison

item.title: Cisco Intersight Add-on for Splunk - v3.1.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

VERSION 3.1.1

  1. Corrected and improved the wording of the help text for the Intersight Hostname field in account configuration to reduce user confusion.
  2. Added backend handling to automatically remove the **“https://”** prefix (if provided) and store only the required domain value, preventing misconfiguration.


Description:
Cisco Intersight Add-on for Splunk is a Cisco supported extension to Splunk platform designed to integrate Cisco Intersight's powerful infrastructure management capabilities with Splunk's robust data analysis platform. This add-on enables IT administrators and engineers to collect, monitor, and analyze data from Cisco Intersight, providing deep insights into their data center operations and enhancing their ability to manage infrastructure efficiently. The Cisco Intersight Add-on for Splunk enables data collection from key sources including Alarms, Audit Logs, Inventory, and Metrics within the Cisco Intersight platform. It also includes pre-built dashboards that provide high-level insights into the collected data, allowing for faster analysis and streamlined monitoring. For more information: Please refer to the User Guide: https://intersight.com/help/saas/resources#intersight_add-on_for_splunk
item.dc:creator: Cisco Systems, Inc.

item.title: Maps+ for Splunk - v4.6.2 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Maps+ for Splunk — Release Notes v4.6.2

Bug Fixes

Fixed: Markers not refreshing on auto-refresh panels

In Simple XML dashboards using a <refresh> interval, maps would appear to update but markers, tooltips, and popup values would stay frozen at the values from the initial page load. After multiple refresh cycles, duplicate markers could also appear on the map.

This release fixes the issue completely. Markers and tooltips now update correctly on every refresh cycle, and the marker count stays consistent with the underlying search results.

The fix also resolves a related JavaScript crash that would occur on the first auto-refresh when clustering was enabled, leaving the panel blank after the initial render.


Description:
The mapping equivalent of a Swiss Army knife for Splunk.
item.dc:creator: Splunk Works

item.title: REST storage/passwords Manager for Splunk - v2.1.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

What's New in 2.1.1

Improved CSV Import Experience

Drag-and-drop file upload

Clicking Import → Upload CSV now opens a modal with a drag-and-drop zone. Drag a CSV file directly from Finder or Explorer onto the zone, or click Browse… to use the OS file picker. Either path feeds into the same preview and validation flow.

Early file validation

Uploads are now validated before any parsing occurs:
- Files larger than 512 KB are rejected immediately with a clear error — no row-by-row processing
- CSVs missing the required username or password header columns are rejected with a single actionable message rather than generating a "username is required" error for every row in the file

Scrollable import results

The Import Complete modal now displays results in a scrollable container, so the outcome of every credential is visible regardless of how many rows were imported.

Capped preview errors

When a file has many invalid rows, the preview shows the first 10 validation errors followed by "…and N more rows skipped" rather than listing every skipped row.


Description:
An intuitive, full-featured, Javascript CRUD interface to the storage/passwords REST endpoint. If you're an app developer looking to securely store passwords for API's, Custom Alert Actions, Modular Inputs or to access any resource that requires a password, this is your tool! Create, update, delete, view passwords, change permissions, sharing and app scope. Ditch your curl commands and upgrade to this simple to use interface.
item.dc:creator: Splunk Works

item.title: Cyber Watch - v1.0.3 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Release notes for CyberWatch v1.0.2 Splunk app, covering the new AI Module (AI Chat, AI Incident Investigation, AI Correlation Optimizer pages), Home page improvements, Incident page enhancements, and bug fixes.

Version 1.0.3

🤖 New: AI Module (3 Pages)
A brand-new AI module has been added under the AI navigation section with three dedicated pages.

AI Chat (/ai)
A fully featured conversational AI interface for SOC analysts to query, investigate, and generate Splunk content.

Multi-thread support — Create and manage named conversation threads (saved to soc_ai index for persistence across sessions)
Thread management — Named thread creation modal with 50-character validation; threads can be archived or deleted
Multi-provider model selection — Supports OpenAI, Google Gemini, OpenRouter, and Ollama (local LLMs via proxy); model list dynamically fetched from the API
Model switcher — Right-side panel shows currently active model and allows switching models per session
Dashboard generation — Attachment menu allows generating Splunk Classic XML dashboards via an AI-driven wizard
Scroll-to-bottom button — Auto-enabled when the chat viewport is not at the bottom
Welcome state — Clean empty-state shown when no threads exist; prompts analyst to start a new thread
AI Incident Investigation (/ai_incident)
An AI-assisted investigation page that performs deep-dive analysis on specific security incidents.

Incident autocomplete — Live search/auto-suggest from the last 100 notables as you type an incident ID
Incident summary panel — Upon selecting an incident, a visual summary is displayed with:
Rule description, security domain, urgency, detected time
First response time and total response time (in minutes) calculated from history events
Timeline of all incident lifecycle events (status changes, owner updates, worknotes, AI investigations)
Configurable analysis depth:
Quick Analysis — Fast triage summary
Standard — Balanced threat analysis (default)
Deep Investigation — Comprehensive IOC and context mapping
Malware Investigation — Dedicated malware-focused analysis using the AIMalware module
Save Investigation — Completed AI analyses can be saved to the soc_ai index and referenced later
Past Investigations sidebar — Shows previous investigations for the same incident, paginated (5 per page), with re-load support
Multi-provider AI — Reads cyberwatch.conf → ai_settings and calls the active provider (OpenAI / Gemini / OpenRouter / Ollama)
AI Correlation Optimizer (/ai_correlation)
An AI-driven tool for analyzing and improving correlation search rules.

Live rule browser — Loads all active correlation searches from Splunk via REST API (/servicesNS/-/-/saved/searches), shows app name and severity badge per rule
Rule trigger stats — Displays alert count for the last 30 days alongside each rule
Analysis focus areas — Configurable checkboxes (loaded from ai_correlation.json) to target specific aspects: SPL optimization, coverage gaps, detection logic, MITRE mapping, etc.
Include notable events — Optional inclusion of 1–10 recent notable events as AI context
AI optimization output — Results rendered as a scored analysis with:
Summary and optimization score
Suggested improved SPL with Copy and Run in Splunk buttons
Coverage gap analysis and MITRE ATT&CK mapping recommendations
Save Analysis — Persist analysis results to Splunk for future reference
Recent Analysis history — Sidebar section shows past analyses for a selected rule
Collapsible sidebar sections — Active Rules, Analysis Settings, and Recent Analysis panels are independently collapsible
🏠 Home Page Improvements
Critical Notable counter — Header stat box now labeled "Critical Notable" (previously generic label) with real-time count and trend indicator
Percentage trend circles — Animated progress rings for Notable, Critical, Electricity, and Gas metrics with trend direction arrows
Optimized base searches — Switched to a single base search per time period that feeds all metric tiles, reducing redundant Splunk searches
Time period switching — Enhanced animation on 24h / 7d / 30d button transitions; previous search instances are properly revoked before creating new ones
Activity Center — Dedicated section displaying recent SOC activity
Correlation Events chart — Chart.js powered bar/line chart showing correlation event volume over the selected time period
Main chart filtering — Filter buttons toggle individual data series on the main trend chart
Real-time animated counters — Number metrics animate smoothly when values change between time periods
Header toast notifications — Status messages surfaced via toast banners within the header area
Trendline sparklines — Per-metric inline trendlines drawn using D3.js
🚨 Incident Page Improvements
Collapsible UI panels — Toggle buttons independently show/hide:
Timeline chart
Filter bar
Donut charts panel
AI Investigation integration — Each expanded incident row now includes an AI Investigate button that launches an in-context investigation via the
AIInvestigation
module, plus a Saved AI Investigations history section
Inline worknotes — Add and view worknotes directly within an expanded incident row without leaving the page; worknote history is fetched from Splunk in real time
Incident change history — Expanded rows display a full change history timeline (status changes, owner reassignments, worknotes) pulled from soc_summary and soc_notable
Search Results & Raw Event buttons — Direct links to Splunk search results and a raw event modal viewer within each expanded row
Status dropdown — Inline status change (New / In Progress / Pending / Closed) from the incident table, with history saved to Splunk
Owner assignment — "Assign to me" plus full Splunk user list dropdown directly from the incident table row
Donut chart sync with filters — Applying filters updates all five donut charts (Urgency, Status, Domain, Top Rules, Owner) in real time without re-running Splunk searches
Configurable pagination — Entries-per-page selector; pagination state maintained independently from filters
Animated loading state — Stacked-cards CSS loader shown while incident data is being fetched
Filter-to-chart live update — Clearing or applying filters instantly refreshes both the table and all visualization charts
🐛 Bug Fixes
Area Fix
AI Chat Fixed API key being lost when switching models — key is now re-fetched before saving model selection
AI Chat Thread creation no longer auto-fires on page load; welcome state shown when no threads exist
Incident Fixed incorrect status normalization (space-to-hyphen) breaking filter matching for "In Progress"
Incident Expand button click no longer propagates to the row click handler
Incident Donut charts now trigger a resize event when re-shown after being toggled off, fixing blank chart rendering
Home Duplicate search manager IDs eliminated; old managers are revoked before re-creating for a new time period
AI Correlation UI container now cleared before rebuild to prevent duplicate elements on re-initialization
AI Incident Analysis depth "malware" mode now routes to the dedicated AIMalware module, not the standard investigation flow
⚙️ Configuration & Setup
AI provider and model are configured centrally via Settings → AI and stored in cyberwatch.conf → [ai_settings] with keys: active_provider, active_model, active_key
API keys are stored securely in Splunk's credential store under realm cyberwatch_ai_<provider>
AI prompt templates are loaded from /static/app/cyberwatch/prompt/ (editable JSON/MD files)
All AI conversations and investigations are persisted to the soc_ai index with sourcetype ai_chat / ai_investigation


Description:
CyberWatch is a premium Security Operations Center (SOC) application for Splunk Enterprise that serves as a powerful alternative to Splunk Enterprise Security (ES). Built specifically for SOC environments, CyberWatch provides enterprise-grade security monitoring, incident management, and threat detection capabilities without the complexity and cost of Splunk ES. The app features an intuitive dark-themed dashboard that gives security teams real-time visibility into their security posture and threat landscape. Advanced incident management capabilities allow analysts to create, track, and resolve security incidents with full audit trails and team collaboration features. Integrated MITRE ATT&CK framework mapping provides visual threat intelligence and helps identify gaps in security controls and detection capabilities. CyberWatch's correlation engine enables security teams to build complex detection rules and automated workflows for threat hunting and incident response. The app features customizable security response playbooks and workflow automation to ensure consistent incident handling procedures across the organization. Built with modern web technologies and optimized for performance, CyberWatch supports distributed Splunk environments including search head clusters and indexer clusters. The app integrates seamlessly with existing Splunk security solutions and provides role-based access control for different user permissions. Perfect for organizations seeking enterprise security capabilities without Splunk ES licensing costs, CyberWatch transforms Splunk into a powerful SOC command center with premium security tools and automation capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Kumar Suman

item.title: Request Builder - v1.0.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

1.0.0 - Initial Release

Features
- Custom | req streaming search command for outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests directly from SPL
- Supports all HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
- Custom headers, request body, cookies, and timeout control
- Secure credential management via Splunk Storage Passwords (Basic Auth, Bearer Token, API Key)
- SSL/TLS verification with configurable toggle per request
- Events enriched with response status code, body, and headers
- Comprehensive search assistant with full syntax documentation and examples

Authentication
- basic — HTTP Basic Auth
- bearer — Bearer token via Authorization header
- apikey — API key via custom header (default: x-api-key)

Included
- Interactive home dashboard with quick start examples
- Full README with installation, configuration, and troubleshooting guide


Short Description:
Make outbound HTTP/HTTPS API calls directly from SPL. The | req command enriches events with API responses, triggers webhooks, and integrates Splunk with any REST endpoint — supporting Basic, Bearer, and API key auth via Splunk Storage Passwords.


Description:
Request Builder adds the | req custom SPL command, letting you make outbound HTTP and HTTPS requests directly from the Splunk search pipeline without any extra middleware. The problem it solves: Splunk has no built-in way to call external REST APIs or webhooks during a search. Request Builder fills that gap by turning every event into a potential HTTP request — you can enrich events with live API responses, trigger SOAR or CI/CD workflows from alert actions, post data to ticketing or notification systems, or pull inventory and configuration from any REST endpoint, all with standard SPL syntax. Key capabilities: - Supports GET, POST methods - Custom request headers and JSON or plain-text request bodies - Cookie injection and configurable SSL verification (with certifi CA bundle) - Per-request timeout control - Authentication via HTTP Basic Auth, ****** or API key header — credentials stored securely in Splunk Storage Passwords, never in plain text in searches - Adds status_code, response, response_headers, and ssl_verify fields to each enriched event - Works across Splunk Enterprise 8.x+ and Splunk Cloud
item.dc:creator: elif kaplans

item.title: AI Query Assistant for Splunk - v2.2.7 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Short Description:
Use natural language to generate SPL queries in Splunk with support for multiple AI providers, query history, templates, and enterprise security guardrails.


Description:
AI Query Assistant for Splunk is an AI-powered app that enables users to query and analyze Splunk data using natural language. It helps convert plain-language questions into structured SPL searches, reducing the learning curve for SPL and improving analyst efficiency. The app is designed for security operations, IT operations, and data exploration scenarios where users need faster and more intuitive access to Splunk data. It supports multiple AI providers, including OpenAI-compatible APIs and Anthropic Claude, and provides configurable safeguards to help reduce the risk of unsafe or invalid query generation. Key capabilities include natural language to SPL conversion, AI provider management, query history, reusable query templates, and controlled enterprise usage with license-based activation. This app is intended for organizations that want to improve Splunk usability while maintaining operational control and security.
item.dc:creator: sevion xia

item.title: Splunk AI Assistant for SPL - v1.5.1 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
Splunk AI Assistant for SPL offers bi-directional translation between natural language (NL) and Splunk Search Processing Language (SPL) with the ability to be fully personalized in context of your unique environment and data. What Splunk’s AI Assistant for SPL can do for you: With Agentic Response Generation, all capabilities are seamlessly integrated into a single chat window. The assistant now processes your requests with advanced reasoning and tool-calling, providing: 📝 Intelligent SPL Generation: Compose what you want to search in plain English, and the assistant translates the request into Splunk Search Processing Language (SPL). This feature helps you write valid, accurate queries, even for complex prompts, providing a strong baseline for your data analysis. 🚀 SPL Optimization: Now generally available, the assistant can optimize your existing and newly generated searches to execute faster and consume fewer resources, leading to quicker insights and more efficient operations. 📖 SPL Explanation: Understand any SPL search in plain English, complete with a detailed breakdown of its logic and purpose. 📚 Knowledge Retrieval: Get answers to questions about Splunk documentation, platform terms, or products. The assistant exposes its reasoning steps to build trust and asks clarifying questions for ambiguous prompts, ensuring more accurate and reliable outputs. Personalization Personalization in Splunk AI Assistant for SPL is an optional feature that generates more accurate and contextual SPL results by considering your unique data, environment, and past queries, while only using non-personal data and supporting role-based access controls. This helps users build better, more relevant SPL searches and distinguishes Splunk’s AI Assistant from generic third-party tools. More Model Runtime Choices (General Availability) Splunk AI Assistant for SPL now leverages models in Azure OpenAI in addition to Splunk-hosted Large Language Models (LLMs) for even better response quality, expanding the range of powerful LLMs at its disposal. (c) Splunk 2026. All rights reserved.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: ThreatMon Incident Management - v1.0.3 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

  1. Takedown request flow fixed — findings table now shows all findings dynamically and takedown button works reliably.
  2. Set Status updates now instantly reflected in the incident list via KV Store.
  3. Incident table visual improvements.


Description:
The Incidenet Integration app is designed to simplify and automate the process of connecting Splunk with external incident management platforms. Many organizations struggle with fragmented monitoring and manual workflows when dealing with security alerts or operational issues. This app helps bridge that gap by enabling seamless incident synchronization, and tracking directly from Splunk. By reducing manual effort and ensuring faster response times, it empowers teams to act on critical events more efficiently and with greater confidence.
item.dc:creator: ThreatMon IT

item.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows - v10.0.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

  • Removed python3.7 support
  • Removed duplicate EVAL-reason_id from [source::XmlWinEventLog:Security]
  • New mapping for field user in Event Id 5140


Description:
*** Important: Read upgrade instructions and test add-on update before deploying to production *** The Splunk Add-on for Windows 5.0.0 introduced breaking changes. If you are upgrading from a version of the Splunk Add-on for Windows that is earlier than 5.0.0, you must follow the documented upgrade instructions to avoid data loss. A best practice is to test the upgraded version in a non-production environment before deploying to production. Neither the Splunk Add-on for Windows DNS version 1.0.1 nor the Splunk Add-on for Windows Active Directory version 1.0.0 is supported when installed alongside the Splunk Add-on for Windows version 6.0.0. The Splunk Add-on for Windows version 6.0.0 includes the Splunk Add-on for Windows DNS and the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory. The Splunk for Microsoft Windows add-on includes predefined inputs to collect data from Windows systems and maps to normalize the data to the Common Information Model.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: VMware Carbon Black Cloud - v2.2.6 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

VMware Carbon Black Cloud App for Splunk v2.2.6

Splunk 10 Compatibility

  • Set python.version = python3.9 across all modular inputs, REST endpoints, alert actions, and custom commands for full Splunk 10.x Python 3.9 runtime support
  • Minimum supported Splunk version is now 9.3 (9.2 and older no longer supported)

CBC SDK Update

  • Upgraded vendored CBC SDK from 1.5.9 to 1.5.10
  • Removed dependency on backports-datetime-fromisoformat C extension, which caused ModuleNotFoundError on Splunk 10.2's Python 3.9.25 runtime
  • Removed dead backports and rpds vendor directories containing non-portable compiled artifacts

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed proxy SSL configuration in alert actions and custom commands — is identity comparisons were silently preventing HTTPS proxy connections from being established
  • Added export = system to metadata/default.meta for datamodels, tags, transforms, props, eventtypes, views, savedsearches, and commands — resolves issue where the VMWare_CBC datamodel was not visible to Splunk's search subsystem
  • Removed leftover props.conf.orig file

Security Updates

  • Updated requests to 2.32.4 (CVE-2024-47081)
  • Updated urllib3 to 1.26.19 (CVE-2024-37891)
  • Updated certifi to 2024.7.4 (CVE-2024-39689)
  • Updated validators to 0.21.0 (CVE-2023-45813)


Description:
The VMware Carbon Black Cloud App for Splunk is a single application to integrate your endpoint and workload security features and telemetry directly into Splunk dashboards, workflows and alert streams. This application connects with any Carbon Black Cloud offering and replaces the existing product-specific Carbon Black apps for Splunk. This app provides a unified solution to integrate Carbon Black Cloud Endpoint and Workload offerings with Splunk Enterprise, Splunk Cloud, and Splunk Enterprise Security (ES). Out-of-the-box, this app provides holistic visibility into the state of your endpoints and workloads through customizable dashboards and alert feeds in Splunk. For distributed Splunk environments, please use the Input Add-on (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5333/) and the Technology Add-on (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5334/).
item.dc:creator: Carbon Black Developer Network

item.title: Cloudflare App for Splunk - v2.3.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

(1) Compliant with Splunk v10
(2) Update the timestamp extraction so it uses EdgeStartTimestamp when present and falls back to Datetime for Firewall events


Description:
The Cloudflare App for Splunk makes analyzing Cloudflare logs easy and provides you with insights on the performance, reliability, and security of your websites and applications on the Cloudflare network. Use these insights to tune your Cloudflare configuration and provide the best experience for your end users. ## The application includes seventeen (17) dashboards powered by a custom Cloudflare Data Models. ##The application also includes five (5) dashboards for ZeroTrust logs based on index searches. ##This application also includes one (1) dashboard for Area1 logs based on index searches. ## By default, the application is shipped with Data Model Acceleration turned off but can be optionally turned on for improved speeds when loading dashboards and panels. ## The application also includes Common Information Model (CIM) compliant search-time field extractions and field aliases. Furthermore, line breaking configurations are included in the application. The Cloudflare App for Splunk is compatible with all Splunk Enterprise 9.x - 7.x releases and supports single Search Head deployments as well as being deployed on Search Head Clusters. ## Dashboards are broken up into 5 categories: Snapshot, Performance, Reliability, Security, and Zero Trust. Snapshot: - Cloudflare – Snapshot (Web Traffic Overview) - Cloudflare – Snapshot (Web Traffic Types) Performance: - Cloudflare – Performance (Hostname, Content Type, Request Method, Connection Type) - Cloudflare – Performance (Requests and Cache) - Cloudflare – Performance (Bandwidth) - Cloudflare – Performance (Static vs. Dynamic Content) Reliability: - Cloudflare – Reliability (Summary) - Cloudflare – Reliability (Detailed) Security: - Cloudflare – Security (Overview) - Cloudflare – Security (Bots Summary) - Cloudflare – Security (Bots Detailed) - Cloudflare – Security (Rate Limit) - Cloudflare – Security (WAF) - Cloudflare – Security Events ZeroTrust: - Cloudflare - Zero Trust - Access - Cloudflare - Zero Trust - CASB - Cloudflare - Zero Trust - Gateway - Cloudflare - Zero Trust - Device Posture - Cloudflare - Zero Trust - Network Sessions - Cloudlfare - Area1

item.title: Supporting Add-on for Expressway CDR - v0.5.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

First public release


Short Description:
Specifies index-time settings to correctly ingest syslog-formatted CDR records from Cisco Expressway.


Description:
This Supporting Add-on defines index-time settings to ingest syslog-formatted CDR records from Cisco Expressway into Splunk. It is to be deployed on your Splunk Indexers (or if you are using a Heavy Forwarder and forwarding cooked data to your indexes, installed on that HF). It is most effective when also deployed with two other apps on the Splunk Search Head(s) or Search Head Cluster: a) Sideview's Supporting App for Cisco Expressway, and b) Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" app. With these pieces in place the Sideview apps on the search head implement a general purpose investigation and reporting solution for this data. NOTE: There are also some references in the app's config to other sourcetypes cisco_expressway_cdr_csv and/or cisco_expressway_cdr_json. These are still under development.
item.dc:creator: Sideview, LLC - Partner

item.title: Druv Prism - v1.2.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

v1.2.0 — Initial Splunkbase Release

What's new

  • Launcher dashboard for Druv Prism SaaS platform
  • Natural language to SPL query assistant accessible directly from Splunk Cloud
  • One-click launch to prism.druv.tech from within your Splunk environment

Requirements

  • Splunk Cloud
  • A Druv Prism account — visit prism.druv.tech to get started

Notes

This is the initial public release of the Druv Prism Splunk app.
Splunk Enterprise support is on the roadmap — contact sales@druv.tech to register interest.


Short Description:
AI-powered natural language query assistant for Splunk Cloud. This app is a launcher for the Druv Prism SaaS platform — install it, click Launch Druv Prism, and start querying your Splunk data in plain English. Zero SPL required. A Druv Prism account is required.


Description:
Druv Prism removes the SPL bottleneck for your entire Splunk organization. Type a question in plain English — Druv Prism translates it into a governed, optimized SPL query, executes it against your Splunk Cloud instance, and returns results with auto-generated visualizations. Zero SPL required. 10x faster time-to-insight. 80% reduction in analyst wait time for ad-hoc data requests. Key capabilities: - Natural Language to SPL — no query language expertise needed. Any analyst, compliance officer, or executive can query Splunk without writing a single line of SPL. - Schema Intelligence — automatically discovers your indexes, sourcetypes, and field mappings for precise, executable queries. No hallucinated field names. - Query Governance — every AI-generated query passes through a mandatory governance gate enforcing role-based access controls, time bounds, and scan budgets before execution. - Continuous Learning — improves with per-tenant feedback and few-shot examples. Gets smarter the more your team uses it. - Auto-Visualizations — results rendered automatically as charts, time series, and tables. No manual configuration required. Compatible with Splunk Cloud. This add-on serves as a launcher for the Druv Prism SaaS platform. After installation, click Launch Druv Prism to access the full natural language analytics experience at prism.druv.tech. Start a free 15-day trial at prism.druv.tech or contact sales@druv.tech to discuss enterprise plans.
item.dc:creator: Udaya Dasari

item.title: Owl Diode Receiver Add-On - v1.2.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Splunk recommended updates
Splunk Enterprise Compatibility
Splunk Web Compatibility


Description:
This Add-On works together with the Owl Diode Sender Add-On (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5554/). It is designed to send data between Splunk servers that can pass through a data diode, while preserving the Splunk metadata. The sender will encapsulate Splunk metadata like sourcetype, source, host, _time into the _raw message which can then be forwarded over syslog via UDP or TCP. The receiver will unpack this and populate the metadata fields as well and restore the _raw to its original state.
item.dc:creator: Owl Product Management

item.title: Owl Diode Sender Add-On - v1.2.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Splunk recommended updates
Splunk Enterprise Compatibility
Splunk Web Compatibility


Description:
This Add-On works together with the Owl Diode Receiver Add-On (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5555/). It is designed to send data between Splunk servers that can pass through a Owl Data Diode, while preserving the Splunk metadata. The sender will encapsulate Splunk metadata like sourcetype, source, host, _time into the _raw message which can then be forwarded over UDP or TCP. The receiver will unpack this and populate the metadata fields as well and restore the _raw to its original state.

item.description:

Release Notes:

-- Sideview's contextual menu pattern now allows a style for a 'menuDisabledLink' for apps that want to inform users that certain functionality exists even if their user account lacks necessary capabilities to use it.


Description:
Canary is essentially an alternate User Interface framework for Splunk. It implements its own extensible view creation slash dashboarding system that other apps can use. It is a descendant of the old Sideview Utils (defunct for years now) but whereas Sideview Utils ran on top of Splunk’s “Advanced XML” systems, Canary does not. For a great deal more information about Canary, see our website ( https://sideviewapps.com/documentation/canary ) NOTE: This app runs in python3 without any problems and without any changes required. Splunk's "Upgrade Readiness App" may erroneously state that our apps do not run in python3 but it is wrong and please contact us if you have any questions about this.

Description:
This app does regular automated Splunk backups for configuration files, kvstore, state + additional scripts if used - locally Optionally on a remote location - via a remote mount - on a cloud bucket (static or automatically via instances tags) (currently implemented AWS and GCP) * Setup * See the details tab for dependencies and setup instructions. Additionally detailed instructions are on splunkconf-backup wiki at https://github.com/splunk/splunkconf-backup/wiki * Support * While this app is not formally supported, the developer can be reached in splunk-usergroups slack #splunkconf-backup Responses are made on a best effort basis. Feedback is always welcome and appreciated! You can also access app repository on https://github.com/splunk/splunkconf-backup

item.title: Infigo SIEM - v4.3.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
Infigo SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) not only has all the great features that you expect from a modern SIEM but also has advanced capabilities like multitenancy and dynamic assets discovery right out of the box. How is that possible? Vast experience! Infigo IS has been strictly in the information security business since 2005, doing offensive and defensive side, consulting, building apps on Splunk since 2012 – Infigo SIEM is the result of our combined experience, born from frustration of using products that underdelivered on their (security and compliance) promises. This is the fourth iteration of our product, the same product that we've integrated in telecoms, financial institutions, air traffic controls, across myriad industries, often in mission-critical environments. We can't afford to build a bad product, not just because of our customers, but because we use Infigo SIEM as the heart of our managed security services. We offer trial (on-prem) and demo (Splunk Cloud) licenses for everybody who wants to try before they buy. You can request a license and find out more about Infigo SEIM at the official website: www.infigosiem.com

item.title: TheHive/Cortex - v4.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Release Notes

Summary

Version 4.1.0 introduces significant enhancements to user interaction and data enrichment capabilities between Splunk and TheHive/Cortex. This release focuses on investigation workflows, periodic data collection, visual identity, and operational robustness.

Deployment Notes

  • Prerequisite: Python 3.9+ (as per UCC standard).
  • Migration Path:
    • From v4.0.0: This is a non-breaking update. Existing configuration is preserved.
    • From v3.9.0 (Direct Upgrade): You can upgrade directly from v3.9.0 to v4.1.0 without installing v4.0.0 first.
    • ⚠️ CRITICAL: If upgrading from v3.x, the v4.0.0 migration instructions are mandatory and remain valid for this version (UCC architecture migration, breaking configuration changes, and new credential storage). Please read them carefully before proceeding.

New Features

📅 New Modular Inputs: Tasks, Timeline & Status

Three new data collection mechanisms have been added to track your cases and platform health:

  • TheHive Tasks Collection: Periodically retrieve all tasks associated with cases for better operational monitoring.
  • TheHive Timeline Collection: Automatically fetch case timeline events to build a complete history of investigations in Splunk.
  • TheHive Instance Status: Monitor your TheHive instances' health, version, and license status directly from Splunk.
  • Backfill Support: Dedicated backfill scripts are available for Tasks and Timeline to recover historical data.

🛡️ Add Timeline Event Alert Action

A new Custom Alert Action (and Adaptive Response) "TheHive - Add timeline event" is now available.

  • Analysts can now automatically or manually push findings directly to a case's timeline.
  • Supports custom titles (with <inheritance> from search name), descriptions, and specific start/end dates.
  • Fully integrated into the Audit Logs dashboard for tracking.

🔢 Automatic Case Number Resolution

The Add-on now handles both internal IDs (~12345) and human-readable case numbers (2392).

  • If a case number is provided in any alert action (Add Observables, Add Timeline Event), the app automatically resolves it to the correct internal ID via the API.
  • This simplifies workflows for analysts who primarily work with case numbers in the UI.

🎨 High-Quality Visual Identity

All Custom Alert Actions have been updated with unique, high-resolution hexagonal icons.

  • TheHive Actions: Official Yellow (#FFC107) branding.
  • Cortex Actions: Official Light Blue (#87CEFA) branding.
  • Optimized with transparent backgrounds for seamless integration into Splunk's Dark and Light themes.

Improvements & Bug Fixes

⏱️ Standardized Polling Intervals

  • Implemented dynamic interval flooring across all modular inputs (thehive_tasks, thehive_timeline, thehive_alerts_cases, etc.).
  • All collection time ranges are now aligned to clean boundaries (e.g., 12:51:00 to 12:52:00), eliminating "seconds drift" in logs and search results.

📊 Enhanced Audit Logs Dashboard

The Audit Logs dashboard has been significantly updated to provide better operational visibility:

  • Comprehensive Action Tracking: Now includes dedicated filters and metrics for all Custom Alert Actions, including the new Timeline Event action.
  • Data Collection Monitoring: The dashboard now tracks the execution and results of the new modular inputs (Tasks, Timeline, and Status), ensuring full traceability of your data ingestion.
  • Improved Success Monitoring: Logic updated to track specific event codes (e.g., TH201 for successful timeline events, CAA-THCA-125 for cases), allowing for clear distinction between successful creations and operational issues.

🔧 Indexing & Props

  • Fixed thehive:timeline sourcetype to ensure Splunk prioritizes normalized _time from JSON payloads.
  • Cleaned up build process to automatically remove Windows binaries (.exe, .pyd), Python compiled files (.pyc), and caches for Splunk Cloud compatibility.


Description:
This TA allows to add interaction features between TheHive or Cortex (https://www.strangebee.com/) and Splunk. It allows to retrieve all kind of information from TheHive/Cortex and to perform actions on these instances using Splunk, from a search or from a predefined dashboard. This TA is supporting only TheHive 5. For having an app supporting TheHive 3 or 4, please check the oldest releases. It's also supporting the Cortex 3 version. More information here: https://github.com/LetMeR00t/TA-thehive-cortex
item.dc:creator: Alexandre Demeyer
